findings.evidence: `rg -n "def IsSubcomodule|theorem isSubcomodule|submoduleCoactionRange" TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Subcomodule.lean` shows the definition at lines 56-58 and only `isSubcomodule_iff` at line 71; no `isSubcomodule_def` restatement exists.
findings.fix: Add an `isSubcomodule_def` theorem, preferably after defining `IsSubcomodule` in terms of `submoduleCoactionRange`, while keeping `isSubcomodule_iff` as the elementwise characterization.
findings.issue: `IsSubcomodule` has no `isSubcomodule_def` theorem exposing its defining `P.map coact ≤ submoduleCoactionRange P` formulation, so users must unfold the public definition to use the range-order API.

summary: The bundled type is no longer the over-exposed subtype from the prior review, but the public predicate API still lacks its direct definitional restatement. Downstream users have to unfold `IsSubcomodule` to use the range-order formulation that the definition exposes.
